title: Subject Analysis: Cross-Term/Grade Averages & Computer Science Marks Fix

requirements.text: In Subject Analysis, for each grade, calculate and display the average percentage of each subject across ALL terms of that grade (not just the currently selected term). This per-grade subject average should be visible alongside or below the per-term breakdown.

quotes: Average percentage of each subject for all terms of each grade should be calculated

acceptanceCriteria: For each grade, each subject shows an average percentage computed over all terms that have data for that subject in that grade.
The per-grade average is clearly labeled (e.g., 'Grade Average across all terms').
The average updates correctly when new marks entries are added.

requirements.text: In Subject Analysis, also calculate and display the overall average percentage of each subject across ALL grades (not filtered by any single grade). This cross-grade subject average should be shown as a summary row or section.

quotes: average percentage of each subject of all grades also be calculated in subject analysis

acceptanceCriteria: A summary section or row in Subject Analysis shows each subject's average percentage computed across all grades and all terms.
This overall average is clearly labeled (e.g., 'Overall Average across all grades').
Only subjects with at least one data entry contribute to the average.

requirements.text: Update the Computer Science total marks configuration so that: for Grades 1 through 7, Computer Science carries a total of 20 marks; for Grade 8, Computer Science carries a total of 30 marks. This must be reflected in all percentage calculations (Subject Analysis, Progress, Combined %, etc.) that use max marks for Computer Science.

quotes: for computer science total marks is 20 marks only from grade till grade 7
only carries 30 marks only in computer science in grade 8

acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/lib/maxMarks.ts (or equivalent), Computer Science max marks is set to 20 for Grades 1–7.
In frontend/src/lib/maxMarks.ts (or equivalent), Computer Science max marks is set to 30 for Grade 8.
Subject Analysis percentage for Computer Science correctly reflects 20-mark total for Grades 1–7 and 30-mark total for Grade 8.
AddMarksForm enforces the correct max marks for Computer Science based on the selected grade.
No other subjects or grades are affected by this change.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Computer Science marks rule applies only to Grades 1–8; do not alter Computer Science max marks for Grades 9–12 unless explicitly requested.
All percentage calculations must use the grade-specific max marks for Computer Science.
nonGoals: Changing the marks rules for any subject other than Computer Science.
Modifying the backend data model or stored entries.
Altering the Combined Percentage, Certificate, or 9-Scale views beyond what is naturally affected by the corrected max marks.
